instrument_inventory.note: The remaining runner families use heterogeneous description and scoring structures; this count is not a claim that 12 scenarios lack evaluation criteria.

released_sample_records.interpretation_limit: These are heterogeneous demonstration records under claude/ directories, not a complete or randomly sampled paper result matrix. Missing status is preserved rather than recoded as failure.

gomoku_reconstruction.public_contract: Five cumulative subtasks expose query, exact deliverables, and rubric text, including DOM/API names, coordinates, dimensions, tolerances, interaction sequences, and expected files.
gomoku_reconstruction.execution: The runner asks the target model for JSON file contents, applies them to a per-attempt local workspace, syncs that workspace to the remote sandbox, executes fixed browser/JavaScript interaction plans, and captures screenshots, videos, DOM/code, logs, and diagnostics.
gomoku_reconstruction.observers: A text model sees the entire public rubric, conversation digest, all readable workspace/eval-space contents, DOM and diagnostics. A vision model sees the same rubric plus selected screenshots/videos and diagnostics. The vision prompt additionally penalizes aesthetics beyond the explicit rubric.
gomoku_reconstruction.acceptance_and_feedback: Each observer converts an integer 0-10 score to pass at score >= 6; the attempt passes only if both pass. On failure the target model receives both observer identities, scores, pass flags, and free-text reasons. There is no separate Claude user-simulator call in this released Game runner.

subtask1_attempt1.feedback_effect: The next attempt changes the exact failed coordinate and spacing values exposed by the text evaluator.

subtask5_attempts.failure: The assistant returned 'Task completed' instead of the required plan JSON, so artifact evaluation was skipped.

gomoku_reconstruction.sample_record_limit: The record has absolute local paths, 102 zero-valued token metadata entries across the released Game/Frontend sample family, and no scenario-level final score field in this Gomoku meta_eval.json. It cannot reproduce the paper's resource or leaderboard tables.

paper_release_differences: The v4 paper describes a Claude-4-Sonnet user simulation agent that performs gap analysis over judge output, rubrics, deliverables, and artifacts; the released Gomoku runner directly templates text/vision evaluator reasons into the next target-model prompt without a separate simulator call.
The paper says visual-task final scores average text and vision ratings; the released Gomoku runner uses conjunctive text>=6 AND vision>=6 acceptance and records no scenario-level final aggregate.
The v4 paper names Claude-4-Sonnet and Gemini-2.5-Pro judges; the released sample Gomoku record uses gzy/claude-4-sonnet and qwen3-vl-235, while runner configuration is environment-dependent.

The paper's Figure 3 reports Gomoku task-1 text/vision scores of 2/8, matching the first released attempt, but the released runner continues to a second 10/8 passing attempt. The manuscript does not identify which attempt contributes to its displayed or aggregate score.

The paper reports task/scenario token and time summaries, nine-model results, and scaffold comparisons; the release contains one heterogeneous claude demonstration per scenario but no complete paper-run inventory, raw result matrix, seeds/repeats, table script, or nonzero comparable token ledger.

positive: Complete 32-scenario/138-subtask specification surface and one runner per scenario are released.
All 32 demonstration meta_eval files parse as JSON.
The release preserves rich per-attempt prompts, model messages, observer prompts/results, artifact metadata, and feedback for the Game/Frontend family.
Dependencies are version-pinned and the repository is MIT licensed.
negative: The README launches ghcr.io/agent-infra/sandbox:latest rather than an immutable image digest.
Execution depends on hosted SII, target-model, text-judge, and vision-judge services and unreleased .env choices.
The public repository has one commit and no visible version history that can identify paper-time repairs.
No end-to-end test proves host/network isolation or accepted-alternative coverage.
The archive includes pycache and .DS_Store files, and demonstration records contain machine-specific absolute paths.
No complete paper trajectories, result matrices, human-validation labels, judge predictions, repeated runs, costs, or table-reproduction command are released.
